transcript: Uh, I can't divulge that information on the phone, sir. It's been UPS policy for, like, twelve years now.
What do you mean I ordered lambs? What the... what are you talking about?
The lamb. You ordered four or five lambs, from the Lebanon Center.
How... how do you know? You don't even know my name.
I do, but I can't say it on the phone. That's just UPS policy.
What do you mean you can't say on the phone?
[chuckling] It's UPS policy, sir, it's been that way for twelve years now.
[chuckling] No. You... if you don't accept your package, you will at least have to pay for freight, which is... ninety-eight dollars.
I don't know what you're talking about.
No... I mean, wha... what address?
I can't... you know your own address, sir.
It just doesn't have enough postage. When would be the best time to come by and pick... drop it off to you?
No, I'm not at a party, I need to bring you your lambs.
I need to bring you your lambs, sir.
No, sir. I'm just trying to do my job-- [beep] --delivering the lambs that you ordered.
How do you know I ordered lambs?
You just said you ordered lambs.
I said it? What are you talking about? Are you nuts? I... I don't know what's the... I don't know what's the matter with you.
[chuckling] I need to... bring you your package, that's all.
All that's the matter is that you won't... accept your package that you ordered, of lambs.
Listen. I'm gonna hang up. If you call again, I'm gonna call the cops, that's all.
Well, there's only-- [beep] --crime, is being committed by you. You want--
